checklist.claim: the readable-sibling precheck fails closed on directory / dangling symlink / symlink loop; an empty-but-readable sibling takes the read-failure arm; exit 0 and no temp leak on every arm
checklist.evidence: orchestrator drove all four shapes behaviorally; each took the intended arm, exit 0, /tmp delta 0

phase3_findings.description: Two diff-added lines (scripts/flip-review-progress-failed.sh header; docs/DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) assert the run-keyed marker shape is 'pinned in lib/test/run.sh'. It was not — every devflow:review-progress run= occurrence in run.sh was a synthetic fixture. A diff-added claim false against HEAD = self-contradicting diff (non-demotable REJECT). The underlying coupling fails OPEN: a seed/FLIP_MARKER drift makes id return rc 2, the helper no-ops, and both flips silently never fire.
no findings; every clause of the newest comment block verified true
no findings; precheck cannot fail open (unset/empty $WORKPAD, symlink loop, TOCTOU all fail closed); no WP_ERR/TMP leak; nothing can redden the required check

fix_decisions.claim_text: the run-keyed marker shape is pinned in lib/test/run.sh
fix_decisions.evidence: Shape 3 flagged (single-source from pr-test-analyzer). Re-affirmed, and the over-grade gate is moot here: this is a Phase 4.2 self-contradicting-diff carve-out, which is corroboration-independent and non-demotable regardless of severity chip. The orchestrator independently verified the claim is FALSE against HEAD (no assert_pin_* or grep in run.sh references the SKILL.md seed marker or either workflow's FLIP_MARKER). Only a fix clears it; a fix was applied and mutation-verified.

fix_decisions.claim_text: the byte-identity assert has a residual vacuous corner if BOTH workflows drift the suffix identically
fix_decisions.evidence: Step 3.5 gate Suggestion. Backstopped: the per-file assert_pin_unique on the full literal (A3/A4) goes RED on any such suffix change, so the suite still fails. No fix required.

phase3_findings.description: The implement-side flip_to_failed shell glue is statically pinned, never executed (the harness structurally cannot run workflow YAML).
Three defensive arms of the flip helper are unexercised: the mktemp-failure no-op, the RESULT catch-all, and the populated-RUN_URL branch. All best-effort exit-0 no-ops.
The `! grep -q "'failed':"` source pin is evadable by a double-quoted key; the comment does not overstate it and the authoritative semantic guard exists in test_python_scripts.py. No fix required.
Empty-body treated as read-failure no-op; fail-closed and effectively unreachable. Independently matches the orchestrator's park-calibration evaluation.
Ready to merge; no Critical/Important/Suggestion issues

evaluated.finding: body guard's 'could not read body' breadcrumb on rc==0-but-empty
evaluated.evidence: Shape 2 requires 'a real input fails with a misdirected message'. _find_workpad_comment returns the first comment WHOSE BODY STARTS WITH the marker, so any comment id resolves necessarily has a non-empty body; the rc==0-and-empty branch is unreachable for a matched comment (barring a TOCTOU emptying between calls). The breadcrumb also prints rc=0, which disambiguates. The shadow's own silent-failure-hunter independently graded it Informational/unreachable. Parking stands.
Not under-specified vs the issue's ACs: the issue explicitly requires 'No workpad Status write occurs on ... the fail-unreadable arm, or the fail-auth arm.'
